cards.prompt: Scrape HSN code taxonomy from ClearTax (https://cleartax.in/s/gst-hsn-lookup) or Seair (https://www.seair.co.in/hs-codes/heading-8708-parts-accessories-motor-vehicles.aspx). Focus on Chapter 8708 (vehicle parts), 84 (mechanical), 85 (electrical). Output: data/knowledge_graph/hsn_taxonomy.json with fields {code, description, parent_code, children[], level}. Target 200+ codes in parent-child hierarchy. This is the official government taxonomy of every auto part sold in India.

Download 6 DGT ITI mechanic syllabus PDFs from dgt.gov.in and save to data/knowledge_graph/pdfs/. URLs:
1. Mechanic Motor Vehicle CTS 2.0: https://dgt.gov.in/sites/default/files/2023-12/Mechanic%20Motor%20Vehicle_CTS2.0_NSQF-4_0.pdf
2. Mechanic Motor Vehicle CITS: https://www.dgt.gov.in/sites/default/files/Mechanic%20Motor%20Vehicle_CITS2.0N_NSQF-5.pdf
3. Mechanic Automobile (Diesel): https://dgt.gov.in/sites/default/files/2025-01/MECHANIC_AUTOMOBILE.pdf
4. Mechanic Electric Vehicle: https://dgt.gov.in/sites/default/files/2024-01/Mechanic%20Electric%20Vehicle_CTS2.0_NSQF-4.pdf
5. Mechanic Auto Electrical: https://dgt.gov.in/sites/default/files/2023-12/Mech.%20Auto%20Electrical%20Electronics_CTS2.0_NSQF-3.pdf
6. Mechanic Diesel: https://dgt.gov.in/sites/default/files/2023-12/Mechanic%20Diesel_CTS2.0_NSQF-3.pdf

Parse the 6 DGT ITI syllabus PDFs (in data/knowledge_graph/pdfs/) using pdfplumber or PyPDF2. Extract per module: system name, part lists, tools required. Build system→parts mapping covering all 8+ vehicle systems (engine, braking, suspension, steering, transmission, cooling, electrical, body/exhaust). Output: data/knowledge_graph/iti_systems.json. Read context/plans/phase2-knowledge-graph.md and memory/learnings.md for context.

From the parsed DGT ITI syllabi (data/knowledge_graph/iti_systems.json), extract diagnostic/troubleshooting chains: symptom → diagnosis → parts needed. Target 100+ chains across noise symptoms, performance symptoms, handling symptoms, electrical symptoms. Output: data/knowledge_graph/iti_diagnostics.json with structure {symptom, symptom_hindi, diagnosis, parts[], system}. Reference the symptom-to-part mappings already in auto-parts-research/03_vocabulary_taxonomy.md for cross-validation.

Pull vehicle taxonomy data from NHTSA vPIC API (https://vpic.nhtsa.dot.gov/api/). Free, no auth, JSON responses. Get all makes/models for brands that overlap with India: Suzuki (Maruti), Hyundai, Honda, Toyota, Kia, Ford, Volkswagen, Skoda. For each model, get years and vehicle specifications. Output: data/knowledge_graph/nhtsa_vehicles.json. Use the /vehicles/GetMakesForVehicleType and /vehicles/GetModelsForMakeId endpoints.

Pull recall data from NHTSA API for Indian-relevant brands (Suzuki, Hyundai, Honda, Toyota, Kia). Each recall maps a specific component to specific vehicle models — this is ground-truth part→vehicle compatibility data. Extract: component name, vehicle make/model/year, failure description. Output: data/knowledge_graph/nhtsa_recalls.json. Use /Recalls/vehicle/modelyear/{year}/make/{make} endpoint.

Download and parse top 10 ASDC automotive qualification packs from https://www.asdc.org.in/job-roles. Focus on: Automotive Service Technician, Two Wheeler Technician, Automotive Body Repair Technician, EV Maintenance Technician, Engine Technician. Extract task→parts→knowledge mappings per job role. Output: data/knowledge_graph/asdc_tasks.json.

Evaluate TecDoc access via RapidAPI (https://rapidapi.com/ronhartman/api/tecdoc-catalog). Check free tier limits. Pull sample cross-references for 20 common part types (brake pad, oil filter, shock absorber etc.) across Indian vehicle brands. Write a decision doc at context/decisions/004-tecdoc-evaluation.md documenting: free tier limits, data quality, whether it's worth the cost for MVP.

Design the knowledge graph JSON schema. Node types: Part, Category, System, Vehicle, Symptom, Alias, Brand. Edge types: is_a, in_system, caused_by, fits, equivalent_to, known_as. Write the schema as a Python module at auto_parts_search/knowledge_graph.py with dataclasses for nodes and edges, plus load/save/query functions. Include a validate_graph() function. Reference context/decisions/003-knowledge-graph-approach.md.

Build the knowledge graph assembly script that merges all Phase 2 data sources into a unified graph. Inputs: hsn_taxonomy.json, iti_systems.json, iti_diagnostics.json, nhtsa_vehicles.json, nhtsa_recalls.json, asdc_tasks.json, plus vocabulary data from training/vocabulary_pairs.py. Output: data/knowledge_graph/graph.json. Use the schema from auto_parts_search/knowledge_graph.py. Target: 1000+ part nodes, 5+ edge types, every part has ≥1 is_a edge.

Write validation tests for the knowledge graph in tests/test_knowledge_graph.py. Tests: graph loads without error, 1000+ part nodes, all 7 node types present, all 6 edge types present, no orphan nodes (every node has ≥1 edge), every Part has ≥1 is_a edge, ≥100 symptom→part chains, ≥50 vehicle→part edges. Run with pytest.

Map the top 1000 unique part names from Boodmo sitemap data (data/raw/boodmo_sitemap_products.jsonl — 371K unique names) to HSN categories from the knowledge graph. Use fuzzy matching and keyword-based rules. Update graph nodes with Boodmo part name aliases. Output: updated graph.json with richer Part nodes.

Generate hierarchy-based training pairs from the HSN taxonomy in the knowledge graph. Use graded similarity: siblings (same parent) = 0.85, cousins (same grandparent) = 0.4, distant (different chapter) = 0.2. Output: data/training/hierarchy_pairs.jsonl. This replaces binary 1.0/0.0 with graph-distance-based scores. Create training/hierarchy_pairs.py.

Generate system-membership training pairs from the ITI systems data in the knowledge graph. Pairs: 'brake pad' ↔ 'braking system' = 1.0, 'thermostat' ↔ 'cooling system' = 1.0, cross-system = 0.0. Also co-occurrence pairs for parts serviced together. Output: data/training/system_pairs.jsonl. Create training/system_pairs.py.

Generate diagnostic chain training pairs from the ITI diagnostics data. Pairs: 'grinding noise when braking' ↔ 'brake pad' = 0.95, 'engine overheating' ↔ 'thermostat' = 0.9. Include both English and Hindi symptom descriptions. Output: data/training/diagnostic_pairs.jsonl. Create training/diagnostic_pairs.py.

Merge all training pair sources into a unified v2 training set: vocabulary_pairs.jsonl + catalog_pairs.jsonl + hierarchy_pairs.jsonl + system_pairs.jsonl + diagnostic_pairs.jsonl + any compatibility/cross-reference pairs. Output: data/training/all_pairs_v2.jsonl. Update the CLI 'pairs' command to include all v2 sources. Print stats by pair_type and label distribution.

Evaluate 3 base embedding models on our 195-query benchmark WITHOUT fine-tuning. Models: (1) sentence-transformers/paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2, (2) BAAI/bge-m3, (3) jinaai/jina-embeddings-v3 (if API accessible). For each, embed all benchmark queries + a sample product catalog, measure retrieval accuracy. Write results to context/decisions/005-base-model-selection.md.

Set up the embedding model fine-tuning pipeline using sentence-transformers. Script: training/finetune.py. Should: load training pairs from JSONL, create InputExamples, configure CosineSimilarityLoss (or MultipleNegativesRankingLoss), train with warmup, save checkpoint. Configurable: base model, epochs, batch size, output dir. Include evaluation callback using our benchmark.

Train v2 embedding model on the full pair set (all_pairs_v2.jsonl from knowledge graph + vocabulary + catalog). Use the best base model from T300. Run benchmark evaluation. Target: 20%+ improvement over base model on our 195-query benchmark. Save model to models/autoparts-search-v2/. Document results in context/decisions/006-model-v2-results.md.

Build FastAPI search endpoint. Endpoints: POST /search (query → ranked results), POST /catalog/ingest (upload product CSV/JSON → embed and index), GET /analytics/zero-results (failed queries), GET /health. Use Qdrant for vector storage. Use the fine-tuned model for embedding. Create auto_parts_search/api.py. Include OpenAPI docs. Read CLAUDE.md and context/PRODUCT.md for full context.

Build a demo UI for the search API. Next.js + Tailwind. Split-screen layout: left side shows 'Current Search' (basic keyword match simulation), right side shows 'Our Search' (embedding-powered results from our API). User types a query, both sides update. Show the dramatic difference for Hindi queries, misspellings, and symptoms. Deploy to Vercel. Read context/PRODUCT.md for positioning.

Set up GitHub repo for auto-parts-search. Create public or private repo. Push existing commits. Add README.md with: what it is, the problem it solves, architecture diagram (text-based), quick start for running pipeline. Do NOT include API keys or credentials.
